description: Quality evaluation suite for zx-shell-scripting skill. Tests correctness, completeness, and security of generated zx scripts against baseline (no-skill) responses.

cases.title: Glob, filter, and transform files
cases.prompt: Write a zx script that finds all .log files under a ./logs directory, filters to those containing the string 'ERROR', counts how many error lines each file has, and writes a JSON summary to ./error-report.json.

assertions.description: Uses glob() to discover log files
Uses .nothrow() or try/catch for grep — files without errors would exit non-zero
Writes JSON output using fs or redirect
Does NOT add extra quotes around interpolated variables
Uses async/await pattern correctly

cases.title: Parallel HTTP health checks with error handling
cases.prompt: Write a zx script that checks the health of three services — https://api.example.com/health, https://auth.example.com/health, and https://db.example.com/health — in parallel. Each check should timeout after 5 seconds. Print a summary showing which are up and which are down, and exit with code 1 if any are down.

assertions.description: Uses Promise.all() or Promise.allSettled() for parallel execution
Uses .nothrow() so parallel execution doesn't abort on first failure
Applies 5-second timeout per request
Exits with code 1 when any check fails
Checks .ok or .exitCode to determine success/failure

cases.title: System monitor using jc for structured parsing
cases.prompt: Write a zx script that uses jc to parse the output of `df -h` and `ps axu`, identifies any filesystem over 80% usage and any process consuming more than 50% CPU, prints warnings for each, and exits with code 1 if any thresholds are exceeded.

assertions.description: Uses jc to parse df and ps output
Parses output as JSON using .json()
Filters filesystems over 80% or compares use_percent
Filters processes over 50% CPU
Exits with code 1 if thresholds exceeded

cases.title: Retry and timeout for flaky deployment command
cases.prompt: Write a zx script that deploys an application by running `kubectl rollout status deployment/app`. It should retry up to 5 times with a 3-second delay between attempts. Each attempt should timeout after 30 seconds. If all retries are exhausted, run `kubectl rollout undo deployment/app` as a rollback and exit with code 1.

assertions.description: Uses retry() with count of 5 and 3 second delay
Applies a 30-second timeout per attempt
Runs rollback command on failure
Exits with code 1 on failure
Wraps retry in try/catch to handle exhaustion

cases.title: Fetch, transform, and write structured output
cases.prompt: Write a zx script that fetches JSON from https://api.example.com/users, filters to only active users (where active === true), extracts their id and email fields, sorts by email alphabetically, and writes the result as a formatted JSON file to ./active-users.json. Handle fetch errors gracefully.

assertions.description: Uses fetch() global or curl to retrieve data
Filters users where active is true
Maps to id/email only
Sorts by email
Writes formatted JSON output
Handles fetch errors (non-ok response or network failure)
